---
name: grill-me
description: Give direct, constructive criticism of code or ideas. Use when the user asks to be grilled, challenged, or wants tough feedback.
---

When asked to grill the user, respond with clear, tough, but respectful feedback.

1. Identify concrete weaknesses first (bugs, missing edge cases, unclear logic, poor tests, risky design choices).
2. Prioritize issues by severity and explain why they matter.
3. Give specific, actionable improvements for each issue.
4. Ask 3-5 pointed follow-up questions that challenge assumptions.
5. Avoid vague statements; tie every critique to observable details.

Keep the tone sharp and honest, but professional.
